drugs.notes: Tablets to be taken in the morning with or after food
In 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride over 120 minutes; max rate 1mg cisplatin/minute; dose banded at 1mg/ml
In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes; dose banded at 100mg/ml
Filgrastim 30 million units once daily for 7 days OR lenograstim 33.6 million units once daily for 7 days, from day 9

dose_modifications.notes: Delay 1 week; if platelets >=50 give 100% with platelet transfusions
Delay 1 week; if neutrophils >=0.5 give 100% with G-CSF
Delay 1 week; if neutrophils >=0.5 AND platelets >=50 give 100% with G-CSF and platelet transfusions; if neutrophils <0.5 AND/OR platelets <50 hold and recheck every 3 days
Give 100% with G-CSF support OR give 75% of original dose
Initiate treatment with dose of 800mg/m2 (reflects local practice)
Discuss with consultant; dose may need to be reduced

pre_medications: Aprepitant 125mg oral (15-30 min prior, day 1)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (15-30 min prior, day 1)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV (15-30 min prior, day 8)
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV (pre-cisplatin hydration)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 IV over 60 min (pre-hydration)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 IV over 60 min (post-hydration)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily Mon/Wed/Fri oral (prophylaxis)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral for 7 days (cycle 1 only)
special_warnings: Hodgkin Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD; ONLY irradiated blood products must be used for life
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before day 1
Regular blood glucose monitoring required due to dexamethasone
Consider formal audiology testing; cisplatin is ototoxic
Patients should drink at least 3 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin
Cisplatin extravasation risk: classified as exfoliant
Cisplatin: mandatory IV hydration required — pre-hydration and post-hydration per local cisplatin hydration protocol. Monitor urine output, U&Es, and Mg²⁺ before and after each cycle.

disease_site: Relapsed/refractory Hodgkin Lymphoma or Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma

drugs.notes: In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes
Intravenous bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes; vesicant - follow NPSA/2008/RRR04 for vinca alkaloid IV administration

dose_modifications.condition: Day 1 or 8: Neutrophils <1x10^9/L (1st occurrence, recovery within 7 days)
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until neutrophils recover to 1x10^9/L; if within 7 days give full dose with prophylactic G-CSF

dose_modifications.condition: Day 1 or 8: Neutrophils <1x10^9/L (1st occurrence, recovery >7 days or febrile neutropenia)
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce to 75% and give prophylactic G-CSF
Reduce to 75% of original dose with prophylactic G-CSF
Delay until platelets >=75 then give 75% of original dose
Delay until platelets >=75 then give 50% of original dose
Withhold until grade 1, then reduce to 20mg/m2
Withhold until grade 1, then reduce to 15mg/m2; discontinue if no recovery
Reduce to 20mg/m2; may further reduce to 15mg/m2 or stop for persistent symptoms

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV (15-30 min prior to chemotherapy)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral (first cycle only)
G-CSF from day 9 until neutrophil recovery >1x10^9/L (filgrastim 30MU/day SC or lenograstim 33.6MU/day SC or pegfilgrastim 6mg once SC)
special_warnings: Hodgkin Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD; irradiated blood products must be used for life
Vinorelbine is a vesicant; NPSA/2008/RRR04 must be followed for IV vinca alkaloid administration
Prophylactic G-CSF recommended from day 9
Consider anti-infective prophylaxis in high-risk patients (aciclovir 400mg BD, co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri)

drugs.notes: Oral or IV infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes; when oral, take in morning with or after food [VERIFIED: Methylprednisolone 1000mg IV pulse is standard in GemP (Gemcitabine-Cisplatin-Methylprednisolone) salvage lymphoma — this is a high-dose steroid pulse, not a daily dose]
In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes
In 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride over 120-180 minutes; max rate 1mg cisplatin/minute. CrCl 40-59: dose in mg/m2 = CrCl value (e.g. CrCl 45 = 45mg/m2)

dose_modifications.condition: Day 1 or 8: Neutrophils <0.5 or febrile neutropenia
Day 15: Neutrophils <0.5 or febrile neutropenia (1st occurrence)
Day 15: Neutrophils <0.5 or febrile neutropenia (2nd occurrence)

dose_modifications.notes: Modify dose so that mg/m2 = CrCl value (e.g. CrCl 45ml/min = 45mg/m2)

dose_modifications.condition: Neurotoxicity NCI-CTC grade 2 or above, or new hearing deterioration/tinnitus
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction to 75mg/m2 in the first instance

pre_medications: Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV (day 1 and 8, 15-30 min prior)
Aprepitant 125mg oral (day 15, 15-30 min prior)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral or IV (day 15, 15-30 min prior)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV (day 15, 15-30 min prior)
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV (pre-cisplatin on day 15)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 IV over 60 min (pre-hydration, day 15)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 IV over 60 min (post-hydration, day 15)
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral (first cycle only, 28-day supply)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (prophylaxis, high-risk patients)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri oral (prophylaxis, high-risk patients)
special_warnings: Hodgkin Lymphoma patients carry lifelong risk of TA-GVHD; irradiated blood products must be used for life
EDTA or calculated CrCl required before each cycle
Regular blood glucose monitoring required
Consider formal audiology testing
Patients should drink at least 3 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin
Cisplatin: CrCl 40-59 requires dose individualisation (mg/m2 = CrCl value)
Cisplatin: mandatory IV hydration required — pre-hydration and post-hydration per local cisplatin hydration protocol. Monitor urine output, U&Es, and Mg²⁺ before and after each cycle.

drugs.notes: Cycle 1 Day 1 ONLY (pre-treatment to reduce CRS risk). In 250ml sodium chloride 0.9%. Start at 50mg/hour; escalate in 50mg/hour increments every 30 min to max 400mg/hour
Cycle 1 Day 8 ONLY (step-up dose 1). In 25ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 4 hours. Inpatient admission required for 24 hours monitoring for CRS. May be extended to 8 hours if CRS with previous dose.
Cycle 1 Day 15 ONLY (step-up dose 2). In 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 4 hours. May be extended to 8 hours if CRS with previous dose.

Cycles 2-12 Day 1 (full dose). In 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 4 hours (Cycle 2) or 2 hours (Cycles 3-12, if well tolerated). If CRS with previous dose maintain 4 hours. Bispecific antibody CD3xCD20.

dose_modifications.condition: No dose reductions recommended for glofitamab
dose_modifications.notes: Adverse events managed with dose interruption, treatment discontinuation, and reduction of infusion rate. No formal dose reductions recommended.

dose_modifications.condition: Mild hepatic impairment (bilirubin >ULN to <=1.5xULN or AST >ULN)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required. Not studied in moderate or severe hepatic impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Mild to moderate renal impairment (CrCl 30 to <90 mL/min)
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required. Not studied in severe renal impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Glofitamab delayed >1 week following obinutuzumab pre-treatment (>2 week interval)
dose_modifications.notes: Repeat obinutuzumab pre-treatment before restarting glofitamab step-up dosing

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment-free interval 2-6 weeks during step-up (after 2.5mg or 10mg dose)
dose_modifications.notes: Repeat last tolerated glofitamab dose then resume planned step-up dosing
Repeat obinutuzumab pre-treatment and glofitamab step-up dosing

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment-free interval >6 weeks after Cycle 2 (30mg dose)
dose_modifications.notes: Repeat obinutuzumab pre-treatment and glofitamab step-up dosing then resume planned cycle

pre_medications: Obinutuzumab pre-medications (60 min prior): Methylprednisolone sodium succinate 80mg IV, Chlorphenamine 10mg IV, Paracetamol 1000mg oral
Glofitamab pre-medications (Cycles 1 Day 8 & 15, and Cycles 2-3, all patients): Dexamethasone 20mg IV (60 min before), Chlorphenamine 10mg IV (30 min before), Paracetamol 1000mg oral (30 min before)
Glofitamab pre-medications (Cycles 4-13, no prior CRS): Chlorphenamine 10mg IV, Paracetamol 1000mg oral
Glofitamab pre-medications (Cycles 4-13, prior CRS): Dexamethasone 20mg IV, Chlorphenamine 10mg IV, Paracetamol 1000mg oral
Tocilizumab 8mg/kg IV (max 800mg) prescribed in advance for CRS management; one dose must be available on ward prior to glofitamab infusion
Allopurinol 300mg once daily oral (TLS prophylaxis, low-moderate risk); Rasburicase 3mg IV once (high-risk TLS patients)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
Gastric protection (PPI or H2 antagonist)
special_warnings: CRITICAL: Cytokine Release Syndrome (CRS) risk - all patients must be hospitalised for Cycle 1 Day 8 (first glofitamab dose) for 24 hours post-infusion monitoring
CRS can occur weeks after infusion; patient must be issued CRS alert card to carry at all times

CRS symptoms: pyrexia, tiredness, cardiac failure, tachycardia, arrhythmias, dyspnoea, hypoxia, capillary leak, chills, renal impairment, headache, malaise, transaminitis, nausea, diarrhoea, hypotension
Patients with Grade >=2 CRS or who received tocilizumab must be hospitalised for next infusion
Tumour Lysis Syndrome (TLS) risk with obinutuzumab and glofitamab; TLS prophylaxis per individual risk assessment
High TLS risk: lymphocyte count >25x10^9/L and/or CrCl <70ml/min
Blueteq funding form must be completed (NICE TA927)
Check hepatitis B status before starting; positive serology requires liver specialist review
Viral screen required: CMV, HSV, EBV, VZV, HIV
2-3L/day oral fluids starting 1-2 days before Cycle 1 Day 1
Tocilizumab: max 3 doses in 6 weeks; do not exceed
Aria regimen built as in-patient on Cycle 1 Day 8; all other days as outpatient

drugs.notes: Once daily; swallowed whole with water at approximately the same time each day. Available as 140mg, 280mg, 420mg and 560mg tablets. Dose rounded to nearest 140mg (up if halfway). Continue until disease progression or intolerance (12 cycles set in Aria).

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity: neutrophils <1x10^9/L with infection/fever OR neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L OR platelets <30x10^9/L - 1st occurrence after recovery to grade 1
Haematological toxicity - 2nd occurrence after recovery to grade 1
Haematological toxicity - 3rd occurrence after recovery to grade 1

dose_modifications.notes: No data available; prescribe only if benefit outweighs risk; monitor carefully

dose_modifications.condition: Non-haematological toxicity NCI-CTC grade 3 or more
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until resolved to grade 1, then restart using dose reduction table above

pre_medications: Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily Mon/Wed/Fri oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
special_warnings: MCL indication: cyclin D1 overexpression or t(11;14); failure to achieve PR or disease progression after most recent treatment; 1-5 prior lines
Ibrutinib: increased bleeding risk; withhold 3-7 days pre- and post-surgery depending on type and bleeding risk
Atrial fibrillation and ventricular tachycardia are known adverse effects; monitor cardiac status
Hypertension is a known adverse effect; monitor blood pressure
Many drug interactions - caution with agents affecting coagulation/platelet function or CYP3A4
Grapefruit, grapefruit juice, and Seville oranges must be avoided
Hepatitis B reactivation risk; check status before starting
Oral chemotherapy NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed
Must only be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist/oncologist

drugs.notes: Once daily; swallowed whole with water at approximately the same time each day. Available as 140mg, 280mg and 420mg tablets. Dose rounded to nearest 140mg (up if halfway). Continue until disease progression or intolerance (12 cycles set in Aria).

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity: neutrophils <1x10^9/L with infection/fever OR neutrophils <0.5x10^9/L OR platelets <30x10^9/L - 1st occurrence after recovery to grade 1
Haematological toxicity - 2nd occurrence after recovery to grade 1
Haematological toxicity - 3rd occurrence after recovery to grade 1

dose_modifications.notes: No data available; prescribe only if benefit outweighs risk; monitor carefully

pre_medications: Co-trimoxazole 960mg once daily Mon/Wed/Fri oral (anti-infective prophylaxis)
special_warnings: WM indication: at least one prior therapy, OR first-line for patients unsuitable for chemo-immunotherapy; WHO PS 0-2
Ibrutinib: increased bleeding risk; withhold 3-7 days pre- and post-surgery
Atrial fibrillation and ventricular tachycardia are known adverse effects
Hypertension is a known adverse effect; monitor blood pressure
Many drug interactions - caution with agents affecting coagulation/platelet function or CYP3A4
Grapefruit and grapefruit juice, and Seville oranges must be avoided
Hepatitis B reactivation risk; check status before starting
Oral chemotherapy NPSA/2008/RRR001 must be followed
Must only be prescribed under supervision of consultant haematologist/oncologist

drugs.notes: In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes on day -6. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg (down if halfway). Irritant.
Every 12 hours (0900 and 2100) on days -5, -4, -3, -2 (total 8 doses). In 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes. Dose banded at 20mg/ml.
Split into TWO sequential infusions of 100mg/m2 each in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 min each (total 120 min) due to stability. Dose banded at 20mg/ml. Irritant.

In 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes on day -1. Dose rounded to nearest 10mg (down if halfway). Ensure urine output >250ml/hour immediately prior to administration. 90-minute expiry after preparation - strict timing essential. Autologous stem cells infused on day 0, >=24h after melphalan. Use oral cryotherapy (ice chips) to reduce mucositis. Irritant.

dose_modifications.condition: Treatment given regardless of blood counts (haematological)
dose_modifications.notes: No haematological dose modifications; irradiated blood products must be used
50%; dose may be escalated dependent on toxicity
May be necessary to reduce dose; at discretion of consultant oncologist/haematologist only

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 2mg twice daily for 10 days oral or IV (days -6 to +3)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily for 10 days oral or IV (days -6 to +3)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 10 days oral or IV (days -6 to +3)
Aprepitant 125mg oral once on day of melphalan, then 80mg once daily for 2 subsequent days
Melphalan pre-hydration: sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml IV over 90 min at 0930hrs day -1
Furosemide 20mg IV bolus at 1010hrs day -1 (pre-melphalan)
Melphalan post-hydration: multiple IV fluid bags per strict timed schedule
G-CSF (filgrastim biosimilar 30MU/day SC) from day +5 until neutrophils >1x10^9/L for 24h or >3x10^9/L
Aciclovir 400mg oral twice daily until day +90
Ciprofloxacin 250mg oral twice daily from day +1 (stop when neutrophils >1x10^9/L)
Fluconazole 100mg oral once daily (stop when neutrophils >1x10^9/L)
Co-trimoxazole 960mg Mon/Wed/Fri (start 28 days post-discharge when neutrophils >1x10^9/L and platelets >50x10^9/L; until day +120)
Pentamidine 300mg nebuliser prior to discharge
Thromboprophylaxis (LMWH) until platelets <50x10^9/L
special_warnings: Inpatient regimen only - HSCT conditioning
Autologous stem cells infused on day 0, at least 24 hours after melphalan
Irradiated blood products MUST be used; TA-GVHD risk for BEAM patients for 1 week pre-harvest and >=12 weeks post-transplant; Hodgkin patients: lifelong
Melphalan has 90-minute expiry after preparation - strict timing adherence essential
Ensure urine output >250ml/hour immediately before melphalan administration
Carmustine: risk of pulmonary toxicity; dose reduction may be needed in reduced pulmonary function
Etoposide: risk of hypotension on rapid infusion; split into two infusions due to stability
Cytarabine: risk of conjunctivitis - consider corticosteroid eye drops
Consider cryotherapy (ice chips) to reduce mucositis from melphalan

NEGATIVE DAY NUMBERING: Day 0 = stem cell reinfusion day (ASCT). BEAM conditioning chemotherapy on days -6 to -1. All negative days are relative to transplant day. This is standard ASCT conditioning — intentional.

drugs.notes: Loading dose: in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 15 minutes. Administered BEFORE the 3000mg/m2 infusion. Urinary pH must be >7 before start and maintained throughout. High-dose IV methotrexate for CNS lymphoma. | LOADING DOSE (500mg/m²): two Methotrexate entries exist on day 1 — 500mg/m² loading infusion over 15 minutes followed immediately by 3000mg/m² main infusion over 180 minutes. Total day 1 dose = 3500mg/m². Both entries are intentional and represent sequential administration.

Main dose: in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. CrCl must be >=50ml/min. Monitor methotrexate levels every 24h from 48h after start of infusion until level <0.1 micromol/L. Urinary pH maintained >7 throughout. Folinic acid rescue begins 24h after start of MTX infusion. | MAIN INFUSION DOSE (3000mg/m²): two Methotrexate entries exist on day 1 — 500mg/m² loading infusion over 15 minutes followed immediately by this 3000mg/m² main infusion over 180 minutes. Total day 1 dose = 3500mg/m². Both entries are intentional and represent sequential administration.
Every 12 hours (4 doses total on days 2 and 3). In 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. High-dose IV cytarabine for CNS lymphoma.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment until neutrophils >=1x10^9/L AND platelets >=100x10^9/L. No dose reductions for haematological toxicity.
50% dose; escalate on subsequent cycles in absence of toxicity

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic: Bilirubin <50 micromol/L AND AST/ALT <180 units/L
Hepatic: Bilirubin 51-85 micromol/L OR AST/ALT >180 units/L

dose_modifications.notes: CrCl must be >=50ml/min to administer methotrexate in this regimen
Do NOT administer methotrexate if CrCl <50ml/min

dose_modifications.condition: Third fluid compartment (ascites, pleural effusion, oedema)
dose_modifications.notes: Resolve third fluid compartment BEFORE methotrexate administration; delays clearance and increases toxicity

pre_medications: Methotrexate hydration: start >=6 hours before MTX; sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol KCl 1000ml over 240 min with 50-100mmol sodium bicarbonate (urinary pH >7), repeated every 12h until MTX <0.1 micromol/L
Dexamethasone 4mg twice daily for 5 days oral or IV (starting 15-30 min before chemotherapy)
Ondansetron 8mg twice daily for 5 days oral or IV
Metoclopramide 10mg three times daily when required oral or IV
Furosemide 40mg when required oral or IV (fluid overload/urine output)
Folinic acid 30mg IV every 3 hours beginning 24h after start of MTX infusion, until MTX level <0.1 micromol/L
Prednisolone 0.5% OR dexamethasone 0.1% eye drops, 1 drop both eyes four times daily, days 2-5 (cytarabine conjunctivitis prophylaxis)
G-CSF from day 6 until neutrophils >1x10^9/L (filgrastim 30MU/day SC or lenograstim 33.6MU/day SC or pegfilgrastim 6mg once SC day 4)
Aciclovir 400mg twice daily oral
Fluconazole 50mg once daily oral
Pentamidine 300mg nebule once monthly
special_warnings: INPATIENT regimen - all supportive medicines must be prescribed on inpatient chart
CrCl MUST be >=50ml/min before methotrexate administration
Urinary pH must be >7 before starting methotrexate and maintained throughout infusion and until MTX level <0.1 micromol/L
Methotrexate levels every 24h from 48h after start of infusion until <0.1 micromol/L
Strict fluid balance chart required; act if positive by >2 kg/L
Third fluid compartment (ascites, pleural effusion) must be resolved before MTX - delays clearance
Transient bilirubin/transaminase rises lasting up to 2 weeks after MTX are expected - do not stop treatment
Persistent hyperbilirubinaemia or grade 3-4 hypertransaminasemia >3 weeks: discontinue
Significant drug interactions with IV methotrexate at these doses - always check interactions
New cycles begin when neutrophils >=1x10^9/L AND platelets >=100x10^9/L (unsupported)
Consider norethisterone 5mg three times daily in menstruating women
High-dose IV cytarabine and methotrexate given as IV infusion for CNS lymphoma (NOT intrathecal in this protocol)

HIGH-DOSE METHOTREXATE: Leucovorin (folinic acid) rescue is MANDATORY. Start leucovorin 24 hours after methotrexate infusion begins. Continue until methotrexate level <0.1 micromol/L. Ensure adequate hydration (3L/m²/day) and urinary alkalinisation (urine pH >7) before and during infusion. Monitor methotrexate levels at 24h, 48h, 72h post-infusion.
required_patient_fields: methotrexate_level_monitoring: Serum methotrexate levels at 24h, 48h, 72h post-infusion mandatory. Leucovorin dose adjusted per MTX level.
creatinine_clearance: CrCl required — HD-MTX contraindicated if CrCl <60ml/min without dose adjustment.

take_home_medicines.frequency: Every 6 hours starting 24 hours after methotrexate infusion, until MTX level <0.1 micromol/L
take_home_medicines.notes: HD-MTX rescue — dose may need escalating based on MTX levels. Refer to local HD-MTX protocol.
